summary: Matched several runtime exception-support functions by replacing the handwritten asm file asm/Runtime/NMWException.s with C implementations for global destructor-chain handling and Gecko exception fragment registration. The PR added a Runtime/Gecko_ExceptionPPC.h header with the __eti_init_info struct and fragment registration prototypes, moved __init_cpp_exceptions from inline asm to C using the renamed extabindex symbol _eti_init_info_, and updated obj_files.mk to build the new C objects. A small matching workaround used an unused asm function to trigger a peephole-off compiler behavior, with review discussion noting it behaves like #pragma peephole off but better resembles original source.
what_changed: Deleted asm/Runtime/NMWException.s, removing asm definitions for __destroy_global_chain, __register_fragment, __unregister_fragment, fragmentinfo, and lbl_804D7058.
Added src/Runtime/global_destructor_chain.c implementing __destroy_global_chain in C with a DestructorChain struct, __global_destructor_chain pointer, and destructor calls with second argument -1.
Added src/Runtime/Gecko_ExceptionPPC.c implementing __register_fragment and __unregister_fragment using a one-element static ProcessInfo fragment_info array.
Added include/Runtime/Gecko_ExceptionPPC.h with the __eti_init_info layout fields eti_start, eti_end, code_start, code_size and prototypes for __register_fragment and __unregister_fragment.

Reworked src/Runtime/__init_cpp_exceptions.c to include the new runtime header, make fragmentID static int, make GetR2 static asm, implement __init_cpp_exceptions in C, and keep __fini_cpp_exceptions in C.
Renamed the extabindex symbol lbl_80005918 to _eti_init_info_ and updated its self-pointer entry to reference _eti_init_info_.
Declared _eti_init_info_ as __declspec(section ".init") extern __eti_init_info _eti_init_info_[] in __init_cpp_exceptions.c to avoid the prior incompatible-section asm workaround.
Updated obj_files.mk to replace asm/Runtime/NMWException.s.o with src/Runtime/global_destructor_chain.c.o and src/Runtime/Gecko_ExceptionPPC.c.o.
smart_moves: Split the old NMWException asm into focused C translation units: one for destructor-chain runtime logic and one for Gecko exception fragment registration.
Introduced a small header for shared exception-runtime types and function prototypes instead of keeping ad hoc extern declarations in __init_cpp_exceptions.c.
Used a named extabindex symbol, _eti_init_info_, to let __init_cpp_exceptions refer to exception table initialization data from C.
Kept the known linker limitation explicit with a Doxygen @todo noting that _eti_init_info_ should eventually be _eti_init_info once the linker can generate that name.
Preserved matching-sensitive behavior in Gecko_ExceptionPPC.c with an unused asm function under #ifndef NON_MATCHING to trigger the same peephole-off behavior as a pragma.
Made internal state local where possible, such as static fragmentID and static fragment_info, reducing unnecessary global symbol exposure.
decomp_lessons: Runtime asm can often be retired by identifying small data structures from memory access patterns: the fragment table became ProcessInfo with exception_info, TOC, and active fields, and the destructor list became DestructorChain with next, destructor, and object fields.

When an asm implementation exists only because a referenced symbol lives in a difficult section, a section-qualified extern declaration may allow the caller to become C while preserving placement constraints.
For matching CodeWarrior output, source constructs that resemble original-source idioms can be preferable to blunt pragmas when they trigger equivalent compiler behavior.
Document linker-driven naming hacks near their use so future linker improvements have an obvious cleanup target.
Replacing an asm object with C requires updating obj_files.mk in the same PR so the old asm file stops contributing duplicate or stale runtime symbols.
Use static for runtime implementation details unless the original binary or other objects require an exported symbol.
naming_conventions: Exception table init data was renamed from an anonymous label lbl_80005918 to _eti_init_info_, with a trailing underscore because the intended _eti_init_info name could not yet be generated by the linker.
The runtime header uses the original-looking type name __eti_init_info and function names __register_fragment and __unregister_fragment.
The local fragment table type was named ProcessInfo and the static array fragment_info, replacing the prior asm label fragmentinfo.
The global destructor linked-list type was named DestructorChain and its callback typedef Destructor.
The destructor-chain global was named __global_destructor_chain, replacing the prior anonymous sbss label lbl_804D7058.
The R2 accessor was kept as GetR2 and made static within __init_cpp_exceptions.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted a full 79-line asm runtime file after matching its functions in C.
Used static asm char* GetR2(void) containing mr r3, r2 to retrieve the TOC pointer for __register_fragment.
Removed explicit nofralloc and blr from the GetR2 inline asm shown in the old version, leaving the compiler to generate the function framing/return as needed for the match.
Added an unused static asm function under #ifndef NON_MATCHING in Gecko_ExceptionPPC.c to trigger a peephole-off compiler bug/behavior.
Review discussion confirmed the unused asm workaround works the same as #pragma peephole off, but the author chose it because it lined up with the original source better.
Used __declspec(section ".init") on the _eti_init_info_ extern to reference data in the init/extabindex area from C.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether the unused asm function workaround worked differently than simply using #pragma peephole off.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Replied that it works the same as the pragma, but felt it aligns better with the original source than a bare pragma.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested formatting the _eti_init_info_ naming hack as a Doxygen @todo comment; noted they would place it above the _eti_init_info_ declaration, but Doxygen was confused by __declspec at the time.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename _eti_init_info_ to _eti_init_info after linker support improves.
Search for other uses of unused asm functions to trigger peephole-off behavior versus #pragma peephole off.
Check whether fragment_info size remains one element or is generalized in later exception runtime work.
Look for subsequent cleanup of Doxygen comments around __declspec(section ".init").
Search for additional runtime asm files that were converted to C using section-qualified extern data declarations.
